title: On the Mastery of Self
author: From: The Dhammapada
content: Better than a thousand hollow words, is one word that brings peace. You are the master of your own mind; do not be the servant of your circumstances.

title: The Fox and the Lion
author: From: The Prince by Niccolò Machiavelli
content: A leader must be a fox to recognize traps, and a lion to frighten the wolves. Flexibility in tactics is the hallmark of the wise.

title: Sensitivity to Initial Conditions
author: From: Chaos Theory
content: In complex systems, a small change in the beginning leads to vastly different outcomes later. Small inputs, large outputs.

title: The Law of Least Effort
author: From: Psychology of Physical Systems
content: Nature always takes the path of least resistance. If your systems are difficult, you will eventually abandon them.

title: The Illusion of Control
author: From: Nassim Taleb
content: We build complex models to manage risk, but complexity itself is a risk. Simplicity is a hedge against reality.
